title: Hi everyone,
author: dyphum4-dotcom
body:
Hi everyone,
​I'm facing a very frustrating issue with Shizuku on my Galaxy S24 Ultra running the new One UI 8.5.
​I can successfully start Shizuku via Wireless Debugging. It says "Shizuku is running". However, if I leave the app open in the foreground (even without doing anything or switching to another app like LSPatch), it automatically changes to "Not running" after exactly 1 minute. The IPC connection just drops abruptly.
​To save everyone's time, here is the list of things I have already tried (and none of them worked):
​Set Shizuku's (and LSPatch's) battery usage to Unrestricted.
​Locked the Shizuku app in the Recents screen (Keep open).
​Turned OFF "Don't keep activities" in Developer Options.
​Disabled Samsung's "Auto Blocker" in Security settings.
​Turned OFF Wi-Fi Power Saving mode.
​Successfully ran the ADB command to disable the Phantom Process Killer: /system/bin/device_config put activity_manager max_phantom_processes 2147483647 (The command executed perfectly without errors).
​Tried Split-screen mode to keep both Developer Options and Shizuku active on the screen simultaneously.
​Despite all these tweaks, the 1-minute timeout still happens. It seems like One UI 8.5 has a new hidden aggressive background killer or a strict timeout for Wireless Debugging connections.
​Has anyone experienced this on One UI 8.5? Is there any other hidden ADB timeout setting or permission I need to grant? Any help would be greatly appreciated!

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/62f3fa9e-5a78-466c-bc5f-5a0a1c4b4bb3

_Originally posted by @kingevil-ace in https://github.com/RikkaApps/Shizuku/discussions/2249_

title: execution problem
author: ocepro490-lgtm
body:
### Requirements

- [x] Shizuku is downloaded from official channels (GitHub release or Google Play)
- [x] Shizuku is not running in a virtual environment or broken ROM (GrapheneOS)
- [ ] (Root users) No Xposed installed / Xposed is not enabled for Shizuku

### Latest Shizuku version

13.6.0

### Version in use

13.6.0

### Mode

adb

### Android version

16

### Device

A065M

### What did you do

Trying to use wireless debugging

### What happened

It's giving an error that Shizuku can't execute because it's trying to kill an old process that apparently doesn't exist. I've tried everything: clearing data, uninstalling and reinstalling, even killing the process manually, and nothing worked. Error message: 

Starting with wireless adb in port 32779...

info: starter begin
info: killing old process...
fatal: can't kill 3906, please try to stop existing Shizuku from app first.

### What do you expect

I think it's a bug because I've tried everything

title: execution problem
author: ocepro490-lgtm
body:
### Requirements

- [x] Shizuku is downloaded from official channels (GitHub release or Google Play)
- [x] Shizuku is not running in a virtual environment or broken ROM (GrapheneOS)
- [ ] (Root users) No Xposed installed / Xposed is not enabled for Shizuku

### Latest Shizuku version

13.6.0

### Version in use

13.6.0

### Mode

adb

### Android version

16

### Device

A065M

### What did you do

I tried using wireless debugging

### What happened

Shizuku is giving an error because it's trying to delete an old process that the USB command doesn't see, and it's failing to delete it because that process probably doesn't exist. I tried clearing data, uninstalling and reinstalling the process, even trying to delete the process manually, but nothing worked. Error message: 

Starting with wireless adb in port 32779...

info: starter begin
info: killing old process...
fatal: can't kill 3906, please try to stop existing Shizuku from app first.

### What do you expect

I think it's a bug, because I've tried everything without root access or a computer.

title: Shizuku gets stuck at "Waiting for service..." on Oppo A18 (CPH2591)
author: Lutfi-zyxxl
body: ### Requirements - [ ] Shizuku is downloaded from official channels (GitHub release or Google Play) - [ ] Shizuku is not running in a virtual environment or broken ROM (GrapheneOS) - [ ] (Root users) No Xposed installed / Xposed is not enabled for Shizuku ### Latest Shizuku version 13.6 ### Version in use 13.6 ### Mode root ### Android version Android 14 ### Device CPH2591_14.0.0.700(EX01) ### What did you do _No response_ ### What happened Body: Hello, I'm having an issue starting Shizuku using Wireless Debugging. Device information: Device: Oppo A18 (CPH2591) Android: 14 Build: CPH2591_14.0.0.700(EX01) Shizuku: v13.6.0 (official GitHub release) Issue: Pairing with Wireless Debugging succeeds, and the notification "Wireless debugging connected" appears. However, after tapping Start, the starter log always stops at: starting server... shizuku_server pid is ... shizuku_starter exit with 0 Waiting for service... It never proceeds beyond "Waiting for service...", and the "Wireless debugging connected" notification disappears a few seconds later. What I have already tried: Installed the latest official Shizuku APK from GitHub. Updated my device to the latest available firmware. Re-paired Wireless Debugging multiple times. Revoked USB debugging authorizations and paired again. Restarted the device. Disabled "Verify apps over USB". Disabled battery optimization for Shizuku. The result is always the same. Is this a known compatibility issue with ColorOS/Oppo, or is there anything else I can do to diagnose this problem? Thank you. ### What do you expect _No response_
